title: Relationships and emotions
explanation:
Relationships and emotions

  • relationship words: trust, argument, support, respect, misunderstanding
  • emotions: relieved, embarrassed, jealous, proud, anxious
  • verbs: upset, forgive, encourage, disappoint

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A good friendship is built on mutual ____. | *trust | evidence | schedule
I felt really ____ after passing my driving test. | *relieved | guilty | delayed
questions.feedback: Relieved = less worried after something ends.

questions.text: They had an ____ about money but solved it calmly. | *argument | update | promotion
Her parents always ____ her to keep trying. | *encourage | sentence | arrest

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: He felt ____ when he forgot his best friend's birthday. | embarrassed
She was very ____ of her daughter after the performance. | proud
A simple ____ caused the whole problem. | misunderstanding
If someone hurts you, it can be hard to ____ them. | forgive
My friends gave me a lot of ____ when I moved house. | support

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: You should show ____ for other people's opinions. | *respect | flood | device
He felt ____ before the interview and could not relax. | *anxious | proud | renewable
Some children get ____ when a new baby arrives. | *jealous | balanced | furnished
The bad news really ____ her. | *upset | updated | revised
